desc:
Along the smooth granite walls, some small closets and a chest of drawers are
situated. A large cavebear-fur covers most of the floor, complete with paws and
head. Right in front of the entrance, a man-sized mirror hangs beside a large
clothes hanger filled with worn cloaks and shirts.
name: The Entrance Hall

desc:
Small, exquisitely carved, ebony lamps hang every few feet around the smooth,
stone walls. Around two large oaken tables stand eight robust chairs,
embellished with delicate inlays of ivory and small glistening gems. On the
floor, a couple large, soft carpets efficiently reduce the echoes in the large
living room. The spiral staircase continues up and down.
name: The Living Chamber

desc:
A large fireplace is situated in the middle of the room just a few feet to the
left of where the spiral staircase ends. In front of the fireplace, two large
chairs stand beside a low table. The light from the fireplace is reflected by
small gems inlaid in the snow-white, arched roof. By the northern wall, beside a
large closet, stands a large bed made of finest oak.
name: The Bed Chamber

desc:
This is a rather large garden full of flowering plants and shrubs. There are so
many different kinds of blooms it is hard to imagine any variety or colour of
flower not represented. Zack, the owner of this garden, clearly must have some
strange fixation with flowers. Most likely a well kept secret to all but those
friends that know him well enough to merit an invitation to his home. To the
north, there is the entrance to a large armoury.
name: A Decorative Garden

desc:
This large armoury has rows upon rows of weapon racks arranged in such a way
that each is displayed so it can be seen clearly, as well as taken down quickly,
in time of need. Beyond the weapons, at the rear of the structure, are suits of
armour, shields and helmets. What is most notable about this collection is the
variety of unique styles, clearly this armoury's owner is serious about his
weaponry. At the centre of the room is a large wooden platform.
name: Zack's Armoury

desc:
All the walls of this room are filled with bookcases, except for the west wall
that leads into Utgbalds bedroom. There is a nice set of comfy couches there,
with a really neat table in between them.
name: Utgbald's Livingroom

desc:
In one of the corners of the room there is a really huge bed. Maybe Utgbald is
looking for a companion that wants to share his bed, since it really is huge.
The walls in here are covered with all different kind of pictures.
name: Utgbald's Bedroom

desc:
A long, spiralling stairwell extends down from the corridor above and leads into
these spacious living quarters. The ceilings are very high, yet well lit from
top to bottom. Small shafts inserted into the ceilings help to circulate the
room, bringing in fresh air from the outside all the time. The floor is smooth,
polished by the wise and skillful hands of master Dwarven craftsmen.
name: A Dwarven Hall

desc:
This large cave is well lit by various size lamps on all sides. Ornamented
shields and shiny armour hang from pegs built into the walls. A sword rack leans
up against the south wall, full of blades both sharp and dull. To the east, the
cave opens up to a large hall.
name: A Large Armoury

desc:
Though at the entrance this hallway looks narrow, from within it can be seen
expanding to a cavernous size. The smooth, polished floor slopes gently down to
the north. The walls are covered in mosaics of semi-precious stones, forming
images of Dwarven lords and heroes. With their eyes made of shining rubies, they
look down at all those who pass beneath them.
name: A Dwarven Hallway

desc:
A stalactite hangs down from the roof, a bright gem suspended from its tip. A
faint light emanates from the gem, flickering and making shadows dance on the
walls. In the middle of the room stands a giant stalagmite masterfully carved
into a statue depicting the stout Dwarven warrior Khozdul. One could linger here
for many an hour, simply sitting and pondering, perhaps smoking pipe-weed and
absorbing the past as if to gain from it strength for the future.
name: Meeting Room

desc:
The walls and the floor of this large hall are made of black stone streaked with
gold. Suits of armour line the walls, glittering in the soft light. A couple of
regal carpets cover the floor. Several pillars decorated with sapphires, opals,
diamonds, and other precious jewels stretch towards the smooth marble roof. The
room is so neat and clean that it could probably accomodate even the fairest of
Elves.
name: Khozdul's Bedroom

desc:
This chamber is inhabited by the dwarf Khazdul. It is a spacious room, yet dimly
lit in comparison to the brightness of the main hall to the west. The light from
various candles bounces and flickers as the flames cast shadows on the walls
from all sides. A large bed sits in one corner, on top of an old, woven rug.
name: Khazdul's Bed Chamber

desc:
The walls of this passage are made of large stone blocks placed upon one
another. The blocks are piled from the floor to the ceiling where they form a
dome. Inside the dome, bricks of dark and light marble create a intricate
pattern across the ceiling. Small bronze lanterns provide the cavern with a
bright but yet warm light.
name: Basement Entrance

desc:
Candles and lanterns light this huge hall which is cut deep into the rock.
Across the western wall hangs a large beautifully carved pine branch. Centered
below the branch hangs an oil-painting of Bagger. Below the painting a fireplace
is situated. In front of the fireplace stands a comfortable chair. From the
midst of the northern wall, an old table made out of teak stretches towards the
centre of the room, and along the table some chairs stand. Axes and shields of
different origin are on displayed all over the eastern wall.
name: Cosy Livingroom

desc:
The solid oaken doors open up into a large, high-ceilinged hall with a strange
construction of metal and glass in the centre of the roof. This dome allows the
light of day to illuminate the interior of the hall. Two statues of bronze
dragons with ruby eyes stand in eternal watch for their master on either side of
the door. In the centre of the northern wall, a well-decorated oaken door gives
passage to another room. On either side of the door carpets can be seen hanging
on the wall. The left carpet portrays a golden dragon on a field of crimson and
the right carpet shows a battle scene on which one man is battling a threesome
of orcs. In the northeastern and northwestern corners of the hall there are
fireplaces, which often give warmth to the place. Near the northwestern
fireplace a comfortable sofa has been placed making it an ideal place for
reading. A pile of books, neatly stacked atop each other, lie next to it. Beside
the northeastern fireplace a large table has been set with eight comfortable,
but functional, leather chairs.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
In the northern wall there are two large windows which allow the day light to
illuminate the whole room. Set between them is a large, very comfortable
looking, bed. On the right side of the room a desk has been placed facing the
door. All sorts of maps, covering all of Arda, are laid out on it. On the left
side of the room stands a peg for armour, covered with a gleaming suit of
mithril chain mail. Next to that stands a weapon rack with a master's choice of
hammers and swords. Two small fireplaces, one in the southeastern corner and one
in the southwestern corner of the room, maintain a comfortable temperature in
here.
name: Rhand's Private Bedroom

desc:
A musty odour fills the grey quarters, which are nothing more but a small room
with few furniture. A small bed, covered with prickly straw, has been placed in
the centre, some of the straw littering the grey stone floor, forming a partial
carpet leading towards the door. The door has a window on each side, not
offering a view yet allowing the light of day to enter. The walls are as bare
and cold as the floor, yet save for the musty odour, the place is relatively
clean and well-maintained. A few niches are set in the walls, each set with a
lantern.
name: Babai's Sleeping Chamber

desc:
These palatious ruins show that before the fall of the kingdom of Arthedain
Arnor Lane probably was one of the most noble areas in the city. Today, many of
the old ruins have been removed, while the pavement has been renewed with white
stone, possibly imported from the White Hills near Forlond. The coat-of-arms of
the Eketta family, one of the seven major houses of Arthedain, has been inlaid
into the pavement in coloured stones, faded over time.
name: Arnor Lane

desc:
This large structure with a loft ceiling is the main meeting and dining area.
Along the walls you see several paintings. To the north there is a passage to a
kitchen and near the south wall you notice a small chapel. Oftentimes you can
hear a minstrel, on a raised gallery overlooking the Great Hall, singing
Pedrag's favourite songs about the mysteries of Arda.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
A small fireplace gives this cosy room a warm glow. In the western corner you
see a king-sized bed, covered in warm blankets. A small wardrobe, filled with
Pedrag's clothes and weaponry, can be seen on the northern wall. Near the
fireplace stand a comfortable chair and an elegant bookshelf stacked with Tales
of Arda.
name: Pedrag's Chamber

desc:
A few ruins, reminiscent of great architecture, remain along this straight part
of Arnor Lane; many of the old ruins have either been removed or changed to be
used for housing. The colourful coat-of-arms of the Orros family, one of the
seven major noble houses of the fallen Kingdom of Arthedain, has been inlaid
into the white pavement.
name: Arnor Lane

desc:
Battle-scarred shields and war-worn furs hang along the walls in this round
chamber. A pair of crossed axes is secured above the doorway, whereas a low,
sombre lantern is hanging from the slanted wall. The lantern slightly brightens
the room and throws long shadows along the room. There is a thick, worn rug on
the floor. The rug shows a barely discernible figure of a brave, scarred warrior
in combat, only his slightly crazed look clearly discernible.
name: Aratha's Domed Chamber

desc:
The lane bends in a wide curve and thus forms a right angle around a ruin that
looks like it was once a magnificant building. The coat-of-arms of the Hyarrs
family can be seen here, inlaid into the white pavement. The coloured stones
have faded over time. A few massive carts loaded with heavy blocks of stone have
been left along the road, they seem to be used to deliver material to build new
housing where ruins have been removed.
name: A Bend in Arnor Lane

desc:
A square rug made of soft, brown fur covers a majority of the floor here in the
home of Logazor the Dwarf. The room consists of a wooden-framed bed, a small
table, and a dresser all carved alike by the same maker it would appear. Along
the walls there are several animal trophies displayed from the hunting skills of
the home's owner. A shelf near the back of the room has several different
hunters knives and other trinkets upon it for show.
name: Logazor's Private Chamber

desc:
Unlike the ruins of once so proud city of Fornost, this surprisingly large and
light house is in perfect condition. It has been built from smooth, large stones
and gives out a safe and cosy impression. A large fire pit used for cooking and
warming dominates the western part of the room. The other furniture consists of
a huge, oaken desk, a group of chairs around a table, and a soft-looking sofa.
There is a bed under the windows, and some paintings have been hung between
them. The most curious thing in this room is a bronze figure of a valiant man,
wielding a shining sword and fighting a horrible beast.
name: Obbah's Corner

desc:
The dimly lit hall, built from solid rock, has a cavernous resemblance. It is
elaborately decorated with several coat of arms and precious gems. Torches
adorning the walls provide a flickering light, and give the home a warm
welcoming quality. Between the torches hang various paintings which alude to the
Numenorean ancestry of Arvegil. The largest of the Numenorean paintings hangs
over the banquet table, very majestic and immaculate. The Lord of the Hall
presides from the throne in the centre of the hall, which is cut from the same
rare stone surrounding you.
name: Arvegil's Stone Hall
